obligations.reason: The visible scope includes external JTAG pins, a jtag_key input, pass_chk signal, DMI request/response handshake, and debug module CSR/SBA controls. Analysis should verify that authenticated JTAG state actually gates all DMI access rather than merely existing as a signal.
obligations.subject: External JTAG/debug requester
obligations.operation: read/write DMI registers and receive DMI responses over TDO
obligations.object: Debug Module Interface request/response registers and downstream debug CSRs/SBA controls
obligations.expected_guard: DMI/debug-register operations should be mediated by the visible JTAG authentication/authorization condition (e.g. jtag_key/pass_chk or equivalent) before dmi_req_valid_o can issue requests or dmi_resp data can be shifted out.
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG TAP -> dmi_jtag -> dm_top/dm_csrs DMI request path
state_conditions_to_check: dmi_access asserted by TAP instruction decode
required_evidence_slots: subject: external JTAG/SimJTAG or DMI requester
operation: DMI read/write/shift response
object: DMI registers, dm_csrs registers, system bus access controls
expected guard: pass_chk/jtag_key or equivalent debug authorization
observed RTL gating on dmi_access/update_dr/dmi_req_valid/dmi_tdo
path/reachability from tck/tms/td_i to dm_top/dm_csrs and back to td_o
source locations
knowledge_used: evidence closure: subject, operation, object, guard, path
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer shows jtag_key and pass_chk but not their assignments or all gating expressions; inspect RTL to determine whether guard is complete.

obligations.reason: Debug control can change processor privilege/mode and expose/control execution. The visible RTL has haltreq/resumereq/cmd_valid outputs and csr_regfile debug state, so the analysis must check authorization and mode-transition guards on these paths.
obligations.subject: External debugger via Debug Module
obligations.operation: halt, resume, enter/exit debug mode, write/read debug CSRs
obligations.object: Hart debug control state: haltreq_o, resumereq_o, debug_req_o, debug_mode_q, dpc_q/dscratch registers
obligations.expected_guard: External debug halt/resume and debug mode entry should require authorized debug/DMI access and should not be triggerable solely by unauthenticated JTAG state or test reset/control paths.
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG/DMI -> dm_csrs halt/resume/control outputs -> csr_regfile debug_req_i/debug_mode_q
required_evidence_slots: subject: DMI/JTAG requester
operation: halt/resume/debug command or CSR access
object: hart debug request and debug CSRs
expected guard: debug authorization plus legal dmactive/hart selection state
observed RTL behavior generating haltreq/resumereq/debug_req_o and csr_regfile debug_mode_q updates
path from DMI control register write to core debug request
source locations
obligations.uncertainty: Need RTL expressions for dmcontrol writes, debug_req_o assignment, and csr_regfile debug entry/exit.

obligations.reason: The debug module exposes sbaddress, sbdata, sbreadonaddr, sbreadondata, sbautoincrement, and instantiates dm_sba connected to AXI. Analysis should check whether debug SBA can reach protected address ranges without mediation.
obligations.subject: Debug Module SBA requester
obligations.object: System bus address space reachable through sbaddress/sbdata and AXI master interface
obligations.expected_guard: System Bus Access initiated by debug should be restricted by debug authorization and any address/range/access-size policy applicable to protected memory/peripheral regions; address, read/write, autoincrement, and read-on-data/address paths should all be covered.
obligations.candidate_path: DMI -> dm_csrs System Bus Access signals -> dm_sba/i_axi_slave_connect_rev -> AXI master port
required_evidence_slots: subject: external debugger/DMI requester
operation: SBA read/write/autoincrement
object: memory/peripheral address range on AXI
expected guard: debug authorization plus address/range/access-type policy
observed RTL: sbaddress/sbdata write/read enables, dm_sba AXI transactions, error generation
reachability to peripherals/ROM/AES/PLIC or memory map
source locations
obligations.uncertainty: dm_sba module internals are instantiated but not listed as source file; inspect available connections and any included module definitions if present.

obligations.reason: The fact layer exposes busy/error signals, response queue, SBA address/data registers, and dmactive/ndmreset controls. Permission bugs can arise if configuration changes race with active transfers or stale responses remain after reset/deactivation.
obligations.subject: DMI requester
obligations.operation: configure/update debug command and SBA state during active operations
obligations.object: dm_csrs state including sbaddr_d, sbdata_d, resp_queue_data, cmdbusy/sbbusy state, dmactive_o, ndmreset_o
obligations.expected_guard: Writes that change selected hart, abstract command, system bus address/data, or reset/debug-active state should be blocked or produce errors while the corresponding operation is busy; reset/active transitions should clear or invalidate stale permissions and responses.
obligations.candidate_path: DMI writes to dm_csrs control registers while cmdbusy/sbbusy or reset states are active
required_evidence_slots: subject: DMI requester
operation: DMI writes to command/SBA/control CSRs
object: active debug/SBA operation state
expected guard: busy checks, error setting, reset/clear on dmactive/dmi reset
observed RTL updates to registers and queue
impact path to command execution or bus transaction
source locations
obligations.uncertainty: Need to inspect dm_csrs case statements around DMI register decode and sequential reset behavior.

obligations.reason: csr_regfile contains the main CSR decode and outputs privilege-sensitive controls. Analysis should verify that address decode, read mux, write enables, and exception outputs consistently enforce CSR privilege restrictions.
obligations.subject: Core software executing CSR instruction
obligations.object: Machine/supervisor/debug CSRs such as mtvec, medeleg, mideleg, mie/mip, mstatus fields, satp, dpc, dscratch, cache-control CSRs
obligations.expected_guard: CSR read/write operations should enforce privilege permissions based on CSR address privilege bits/current mode (including umode_i and supervisor/machine state) and should raise read/write access exceptions rather than updating or exposing protected CSRs.
obligations.candidate_path: csr_regfile CSR read/write decode for machine/supervisor/user-visible CSRs
state_conditions_to_check: attempted access to machine/supervisor/debug CSR addresses
required_evidence_slots: subject: executing instruction/current CPU privilege mode
operation: CSR read/write
object: protected CSR/register/control output
expected guard: CSR privilege check and write/read exception
observed RTL: read_access_exception, csr_we, case decode, write update condition
source locations for decode and update
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer lists umode_i but not full privilege-mode inputs/types; inspect csr_regfile for how current privilege is represented.

obligations.reason: Debug CSRs are visible in csr_regfile and affect control flow/debug state. Analysis should check that non-debug execution cannot access or alter them and that DRET behavior is guarded by debug_mode_q.
obligations.subject: Core software and external debugger
obligations.operation: read/write debug CSRs and exit via dret
obligations.expected_guard: Debug-only CSRs such as dpc and dscratch should be readable/writable only in debug mode or through properly authorized debug mechanisms; normal CSR access outside debug mode should be denied.
obligations.candidate_path: csr_regfile debug CSR access and debug-mode state transitions
required_evidence_slots: subject: core CSR instruction vs debug requester
operation: CSR read/write/dret
object: dpc/dscratch/debug state
expected guard: debug_mode_q and/or authorized debug entry
observed RTL conditions in read/write decode and dret handling
source locations
obligations.uncertainty: Need CSR address cases and sequential updates for dpc/dscratch/debug_mode_q.

obligations.reason: The CSR regfile directly drives translation and access-policy outputs. Improper CSR guards or unsafe mode-transition logic could allow lower privilege code to disable or weaken memory protections.
obligations.subject: Core software executing CSR instruction
obligations.operation: configure memory translation and privilege policy
obligations.object: MMU and privilege-policy outputs: satp_ppn_o, asid_o, en_translation_o, en_ld_st_translation_o, sum_o, mxr_o, tvm_o, tw_o, tsr_o, mprv
obligations.expected_guard: Only sufficiently privileged software should configure translation and supervisor memory access policy (satp, SUM, MXR, TVM, TW, TSR, MPRV/MPP-related state); lower privilege attempts should be denied and mode transitions should update effective permissions safely.
required_evidence_slots: subject: software at current privilege level
operation: CSR write/read and return-from-trap mode update
object: MMU/access policy state
expected guard: machine/supervisor privilege checks and legal mode transitions
observed RTL: CSR decode, write masks, output assignments, mret/sret effects
source locations
obligations.uncertainty: Privilege representation beyond umode_i must be identified in the RTL.

obligations.reason: Trap routing and return-target CSRs control privilege transitions and execution redirection. Analysis should check that CSR permissions are enforced consistently at read mux, write update, and return path.
obligations.subject: Core software executing CSR/trap-return instruction
obligations.operation: configure trap/interrupt routing and exception return targets
obligations.object: mtvec_q, stvec_q, medeleg_q, mideleg_q, mie_q, mip_q, mepc_q, sepc_q, mcause_q, scause_q, trap_vector_base_o, epc_o
obligations.expected_guard: Writes to trap vectors, delegation registers, interrupt enable/pending state, and EPC/cause registers should be restricted to the appropriate privilege level and should not be writable by unprivileged software.
required_evidence_slots: subject: core software/current privilege
operation: CSR read/write, mret/sret
object: trap vector/delegation/EPC/interrupt CSRs
expected guard: machine/supervisor privilege checks
observed RTL conditions for writes and outputs
source locations
obligations.uncertainty: Need details of CSR decode and exception handling blocks.

obligations.reason: The peripheral wrapper exposes multiple APB bridges and security-relevant signals including jtag_key output, pkey_loc_o, ROM2, and AES-related APB signals. Analysis should verify whether protected registers/keys are readable or writable by any bus master without privilege mediation.
obligations.subject: SoC bus master accessing peripheral address map
obligations.object: ROM2/pkey_loc_o/jtag_key, AES APB registers, packet APB registers, PLIC configuration
obligations.expected_guard: MMIO accesses to security-sensitive peripheral registers or ROM/key outputs should be mediated by address decode plus any required privilege/security attributes from the bus; writes to read-only assets and reads of secret/key state should be blocked or return safe values.
obligations.candidate_path: APB/AXI peripheral wrappers in ariane_peripherals to protected peripherals (ROM2 pkey, AES, packet, PLIC)
state_conditions_to_check: AXI aw/ar prot signals where present
required_evidence_slots: subject: AXI/APB bus master
operation: read/write peripheral register
object: key/ROM/AES/PLIC/packet protected register
expected guard: address decode plus privilege/security attribute or read-only/write-only policy
observed RTL decode and prdata/rdata/wdata behavior
source locations
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer is truncated for later peripheral signals; inspect full ariane_peripherals for exact address map and guards.

obligations.reason: Visible facts show a ROM2 instance with write-enable, pkey_loc_o, and a jtag_key output feeding DMI/JTAG authorization. The analysis should check whether the authentication key source can be modified or disclosed through ordinary bus access.
obligations.subject: SoC bus master and JTAG authenticator
obligations.operation: read/write key-related ROM/register and use key for JTAG auth
obligations.object: ROM2 contents/read data, pkey_loc_o, jtag_key signal used by dmi_jtag
obligations.expected_guard: If ROM2 stores or derives a JTAG key/public-key location or other security token, writes should be blocked or tightly controlled and reads should not expose secret material to unprivileged MMIO paths; jtag_key should not be controllable by untrusted bus writes unless explicitly intended and guarded.
obligations.candidate_path: rom2 module and ariane_peripherals pkey/jtag_key connection
required_evidence_slots: subject: bus master and external JTAG requester
operation: write/read ROM2 or key-related signals
object: jtag_key/pkey_loc_o/ROM contents
expected guard: read-only ROM semantics, privilege/lock guard for key material, non-exposure of secrets
observed RTL: rom2 we_i handling, rdata_o contents, ariane_peripherals wiring to jtag_key
source locations
obligations.uncertainty: Need to inspect whether ROM2 is actually writable and whether pkey/jtag_key is secret or public policy data.

obligations.reason: Multiple debug modules expose testmode_i, and the TAP includes a DFT clock mux. Test paths are common bypass sources; analysis should verify that testmode does not enable protected debug access without proper lifecycle/authorization.
obligations.subject: Test interface or testmode signal source
obligations.object: DMI access control, JTAG TDO path, test clock mux, debug reset/active state
obligations.expected_guard: testmode_i should not bypass authentication, force DMI access, expose TDO data, or alter debug reset/clocking in a way that grants debug access in normal operation; any test-only bypass should be conditioned on lifecycle/test authorization if visible.
obligations.candidate_path: testmode_i paths in dmi_jtag_tap/dmi_jtag/dm_top/dm_csrs clock/reset/test muxing
required_evidence_slots: subject: test interface/testmode controller
operation: bypass/enable debug access or data output
object: DMI/JTAG access and debug module state
expected guard: lifecycle/test authorization or restriction to non-production
observed RTL: testmode_i use in muxes/resets/access conditions
source locations
obligations.uncertainty: No lifecycle controller appears in fact layer; determine whether testmode is only a clock-mux control or also an access bypass.

obligations.reason: The visible scope has multiple resets (rst_ni, trst_ni, dmi_rst_ni, dmi_reset, ndmreset). Analysis should ensure reset interactions do not produce transient or persistent unauthorized debug access or stale data exposure.
obligations.subject: Reset/JTAG reset source
obligations.object: pass_chk/auth state, dmi_req/resp state, dmactive/ndmreset, sbaddr/sbdata, debug_mode_q, CSR privilege state, ROM/key outputs
obligations.expected_guard: Reset should place permission/security state in a safe default: debug inactive unless authorized, no stale DMI responses or SBA data exposed, protected CSR state cleared or initialized safely, and key/authentication state not left in a permissive value.
obligations.candidate_path: reset paths across DMI/JTAG/debug CSRs and csr_regfile security state
required_evidence_slots: subject: reset controller or external JTAG reset
operation: reset/clear state
object: security/permission state and data-bearing registers
expected guard: safe reset defaults and stale-data clearing
observed RTL reset assignments and cross-domain reset propagation
source locations
obligations.uncertainty: Need sequential blocks and reset polarity interactions for all debug and CSR modules.
